framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Open-source a tool other developers depend on
paths.what_it_is: Build and publicly maintain a library, framework, or CLI tool that solves a real problem well enough that other developers adopt it into their own projects.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can build something well enough that other developers choose to depend on it in their own projects, not just ship code that works for you.

paths.path: Teach what you're learning, in public, in writing
paths.what_it_is: Publish clear, technically rigorous explanations of web development concepts on a personal blog or newsletter — not just working code, but the reasoning behind it, written for other developers to learn from.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you understand the reasoning behind your code well enough to explain it to someone else, not just make it work.

paths.path: Ship small web products fast and repeatedly, in public
paths.what_it_is: Build and launch small, real, revenue-generating web products on a tight solo timeline (days to weeks, not quarters), sharing the build and the numbers publicly as you go.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can take an idea to a real, working, revenue-generating product on a short timeline, repeatedly — not just talk about shipping.
